bead.title: Runtime signal collector: status, quota, recent latency → runtime cache
bead.description:
Collect volatile per-provider runtime signals (status, quota
remaining, recent p50 latency) and write them to the runtime tier of
M1's cache. Consumed by M2's snapshot assembler at read time to set
KnownModel.Status, .QuotaRemaining, .RecentP50Latency.

## Public API sketch

```go
package runtimesignals

type Signal struct {
    Provider          string
    Status            modelregistry.ModelStatus
    QuotaRemaining    *int        // nil when unknown
    QuotaResetAt      *time.Time
    RecentP50Latency  time.Duration
    LastSuccessAt     *time.Time
    LastErrorAt       *time.Time
    LastErrorMsg      string
    RecordedAt        time.Time
}

func Collect(ctx context.Context, providerName string, cfg *config.ProviderConfig) (Signal, error)
```

## Sources per provider type

- **openrouter, openai-compat**: parse rate-limit headers from any
  recent response (already plumbed via commit 7776890e
  `quota_exhausted`); fetch `/usage` if available
- **claude-subscription**: existing `quota_cache.go` code in
  `internal/harnesses/claude/`; expose its data through this
  surface
- **codex-subscription**: same for `internal/harnesses/codex/quota_cache.go`
- **gemini-subscription**: same for `internal/harnesses/gemini/quota_cache.go`
- **local providers (ds4, llama-server, omlx)**: status only (alive
  check via /v1/models or /props ping); no quota concept

## Aggregation

p50 latency comes from a sliding-window in-memory counter (recent N
requests per provider). On crash/restart, signal is reset (cache file
is empty until next collection). Acceptable — runtime signals are
short-TTL by design.

## Cache integration

Each provider gets its own `runtime/<provider>.json` file in M1's
cache. 5-minute TTL. Stale-while-revalidate: snapshot reads return
immediately, background refresh fires.

## In-scope files

- internal/runtimesignals/signal.go
- internal/runtimesignals/collect.go — per-provider-type collectors
- internal/runtimesignals/headers.go — rate-limit header parsing
  (extends commit 7776890e)
- internal/runtimesignals/latency.go — sliding-window p50
- internal/runtimesignals/signal_test.go
- internal/runtimesignals/collect_test.go

## Depends on

- M0 (ADR-012) accepted
- M1 (cache infrastructure)
- Existing `internal/harnesses/{claude,codex,gemini}/quota_cache.go`
  (consumed, not modified — this bead exposes their data uniformly)

## Out of scope

- Snapshot assembly (M2).
- CLI (M4).
- Persistent historical metrics (out-of-scope per ADR-012; this is
  current-state only).
bead.acceptance:
1. `go test ./internal/runtimesignals/...` passes.
2. Collect() against a mock OR provider with rate-limit headers
   parses QuotaRemaining correctly.
3. Collect() against a mock claude harness reads the existing
   quota_cache.go data.
4. p50 latency sliding window correctly aggregates a stream of
   requests (table test).
5. Runtime cache file path is `runtime/<provider>.json` and the
   data round-trips through M1's cache API.
6. `go vet ./...` passes.

governing.title: ADR-012: Per-Source On-Disk Cache for Discovery + Runtime Signals
